gandhakuṭiyā.meaning: from (or of) the perfumed hut (the Buddha's dwelling)
breakdown: Cabin, hut: the gandhakuṭi is the Buddha's own dwelling, the "perfumed hut".

gandhamācare.meaning: should wear perfume; would use scent
gandhamācare.gram: verb phrase fused by sandhi; accusative noun + 3rd person singular optative of ācarati
breakdown: Should perform, practise, wear (optative of ācarati).

gandhavicāro.meaning: pondering over smells; sustained thought concerning odour

gandhā.gram: masculine noun, nominative plural or ablative singular (√ghā)

breakdown: Absolutive ending: gam + tvā > gantvā, having gone.

gantvādhikaraṇaṁ.meaning: having gone, the performing (of it); having gone and so doing
gantvādhikaraṇaṁ.gram: gerund + neuter noun, fused by sandhi
breakdown: Prefix: over, concerning. Sandhi: gantvā + adhi- > gantvādhi-.

garahaṁ.gram: present participle of garahati, masculine nominative singular; also blame (noun), accusative

garaheyyuṁ.meaning: they would censure; they would reproach
garaheyyuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural optative of garahati

gatayobbanaṁ.meaning: past one's youth; whose youth has gone
breakdown: Gone, gone beyond (past participle of gacchati).

gatimantānaṁ.meaning: of the clever; of those possessed of good sense

gayhamānaṁ.gram: present passive participle of gayhati (√gah), accusative singular
breakdown: Passive stem of gaṇhāti, to take (√gah).

gaṇanapathaṁ.meaning: the range of counting; the path of calculation, measurable period
breakdown: Path, range (accusative): what lies within the range of counting.

gaṇhi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of gaṇhāti (√gah)
breakdown: Present stem of gaṇhāti, to take (√gah).

gaṇhitthā.gram: verb, 2nd person plural aorist of gaṇhāti
breakdown: Aorist stem of gaṇhāti, to take (√gah).

gedhaṁ.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular, from gijjhati (√gidh)
breakdown: Root meaning to crave, be greedy (gidh > gedh).

ghammābhitattassa.meaning: of one tormented by the heat; exhausted by the hot season
breakdown: Tormented, scorched by (past participle of abhitapati). Sandhi: ghamma + abhi- > ghammābhi-.

ghāna.gram: neuter noun, from ghāyati (√ghā, to smell)
breakdown: Noun-forming suffix: the organ of smelling.
Clothing, covering (the doubled cch is sandhi doubling).
Clothing, covering (the doubled cch is sandhi doubling).

ghātayeyya.meaning: would cause to kill; should have (someone) killed
ghātayeyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative, causative of hanati/ghāteti (√ghaṭ)
breakdown: Causative stem: to cause to kill.

gihiparisāya.meaning: in the assembly of householders; among the lay gathering

breakdown: Possessive suffix: one who has a house.

gimhe.meaning: in the hot season; in summer

breakdown: Of the hot season (genitive plural of gimha).

goghātako.gram: masculine agent compound noun, nominative singular

gopālako.gram: masculine agent compound noun, nominative singular (diminutive of gopāla)

gotamīsuttavaṇṇanā.meaning: the commentary on the Gotamī Sutta
breakdown: Gotamī (Mahāpajāpatī Gotamī, the Buddha's foster mother).
gotamīsuttavaṇṇanā.goenka: The Gotamīsuttavaṇṇanā explains that the additional rules (for nuns) are made known for the purpose of preventing transgression before any incident arises: if they had not been made known, then, because women had been ordained, the true Dhamma would not have been maintained for five hundred years.

gotrabhuto.meaning: one in a fit state to receive sanctification; one entering the lineage (of the noble ones)
breakdown: Become, being: one who has become of the lineage.

gottatthaddho.meaning: proud of clan; selfish, hardened about lineage

guttadvāro.meaning: with guarded (sense-)doors; whose doors are protected
guttadvāro.goenka: Guttadvāro describes one who keeps guard over the sense-doors. In Klaus's presentation, two lessons explain in more detail how one keeps guard over one's sense-doors: the Indriyabhāvanāsutta — Mastering the Sense-faculties — and the Dhammānupassanā āyatanapabbaṁ — Understanding the Contents of the Mind, the Section on the Sense Spheres.

gutto.gram: past participle of gopeti (√gup), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Past participle suffix: gup + ta > gutta.

guyhamassa.meaning: his secret; the secret of him
guyhamassa.gram: neuter noun + genitive pronoun, fused by sandhi
breakdown: Secret, what is to be hidden (√guh); -ṁ > -m- before a vowel.

guyhañca.gram: potential participle of gūhati (√guh), neuter singular + particle ca
breakdown: Secret, what should be hidden (√guh + ya); -ṁ > -ñ- before ca.

guṇādhikānaṁ.meaning: of those superior in qualities; of the ones exceeding in virtues
breakdown: Exceeding, superior. Sandhi: guṇa + adhika > guṇādhika.

guṇūpetaṁ.meaning: endowed with qualities; possessed of virtues
breakdown: Endowed with, furnished with (upa + √i, past participle). Sandhi: guṇa + upeta > guṇūpeta.

gāhāpenti.meaning: they cause to be taken; they deliver to
gāhāpenti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, causative of gaṇhāti (√gah)
breakdown: Double causative stem of √gah: to cause to take.

gāhāpessanti.meaning: they will cause to be taken; they will deliver to
gāhāpessanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural future, causative of gaṇhāti (√gah)
breakdown: Double causative stem of √gah: to cause to take.

gāmadhammā.meaning: the way of the village; low, vulgar practice
gāmadhammā.gram: masculine compound noun (derogatory), plural or ablative singular
breakdown: Nature, practice, way: things having the vulgar nature.

gāmaghātampi.meaning: even the plundering of a village; also village-destruction
gāmaghātampi.gram: masculine compound noun, accusative singular + enclitic particle

gāmantasambhatesu.meaning: among the stores laid up near the village
breakdown: Near, vicinity, edge: gāmanta, the neighbourhood of the village.
Stores, provisions gathered (past participle of sambharati), locative plural.
Leader (from √nī, to lead): the one who leads the village.
Suffix of connection: one belonging to or overseeing the village.

gāravo.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular, from garu (√gar)
breakdown: Heavy; venerable, worthy of respect (lengthened to gārav-).
Abstract noun suffix: the attitude of respect.

gāvo.gram: masculine noun, nominative/accusative plural of go
